name: Grandmother's Molasses Taffy
recipeInstructions:
Tire a la Melasse de Grand-Mère
Quebec's sweet tooth is often satisfied by this old-fashioned candy made
from molasses and sugar. This recipe belongs to Marguerite Legault, a
member of Les Fermières de Thuroso, a rural woman's group in the town on
the Ottawa River.
1. In a medium, heavy saucepan, combine molasses, granulated sugar, brown sugar,
and water.
2. Heat mixture over medium heat until it reaches boiling point.
3. Stir to dissolve sugars completely.
4. Continue boiling without stirring until the syrup reaches 260°F (125°C) on
a candy thermometer, indicating the hard ball stage.
5. Remove from heat and stir in butter, baking soda, and salt.
6. Immediately pour the mixture onto a buttered marble slab or baking sheet.
7. Allow to cool slightly until it becomes firm enough to handle.
8. When ready, dip hands in butter to prevent sticking.
9. Gather the taffy into a ball and begin pulling gently between your hands,
stretching and folding repeatedly.
10. Continue this process for about 5 to 15 minutes until the taffy turns
lighter in color.
11. Once finished, stretch the taffy into a rope of desired thickness (about 1 inch).
12. Using buttered scissors, cut the rope into pieces.
13. Wrap each piece individually in waxed paper.
Makes about 48 pieces.
